mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching labor market inflection point, prompting algorithmic risk-off positioning that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the trigger fails to predict a volatility spike exceeding 20% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between manufacturing output and housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks cooling while residential investment remains resilient, a configuration that historically precedes equity outperformance as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: The spread between INDPRO and HOUST percentiles shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02), or equity returns turn negative when INDPRO falls below HOUST growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates exceed 4.5%, sticky inflation readings force the market to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL prints below 2.5% core while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5%, yet SPX forward returns remain positive, invalidating the inflation-restriction transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances reflect sustained consumer liquidity and willingness to borrow, providing a direct tailwind to corporate earnings expectations and lifting S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth decelerates while SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive, or the correlation between RSAFS momentum and equity returns falls below 0.1 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A breakdown in the industrial production index below 105 signals a contraction in real economic activity that typically precedes earnings downgrades, depressing S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO remains below 105 for consecutive months while SPX 60-day forward returns average above 5%, breaking the supply-demand contraction equity link.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and a rising federal funds rate indicate an overheating labor market that forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately crowding out equity risk premiums and reducing forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS both rise, yet SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of observations, indicating the labor-monetary feedback loop does not transmit to equity valuations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5% indicates a hard economic slowdown that triggers corporate margin compression and earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 prints below 1.5% for two consecutive quarters while SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive, invalidating the growth-recession equity transmission channel.
